file.content:
"""Output types for structured generation and regex DSL."""

from dataclasses import dataclass, is_dataclass
from enum import Enum, EnumMeta
from typing import Union

from jsonschema import Draft202012Validator as Validator
from jsonschema.exceptions import SchemaError
from pydantic import BaseModel, TypeAdapter
from typing_extensions import _TypedDictMeta  # type: ignore

from . import airports, countries, locale
from outlines.types.dsl import (
    Regex,
    CFG,
    FSM,
    JsonSchema,
    regex,
    cfg,
    fsm,
    json_schema,
    optional,
    either,
    exactly,
    at_least,
    at_most,
    between,
    zero_or_more,
    one_or_more,
    # deprecated
    repeat,
    times,
)


# Python types
string = Regex(r'"[^"]*"')
integer = Regex(r"[+-]?(0|[1-9][0-9]*)")
boolean = Regex("(True|False)")
number = Regex(rf"{integer.pattern}(\.[0-9]+)?([eE][+-][0-9]+)?")
date = Regex(r"(\d{4})-(0[1-9]|1[0-2])-([0-2][0-9]|3[0-1])")
time = Regex(r"([0-1][0-9]|2[0-3]):([0-5][0-9]):([0-5][0-9])")
datetime = Regex(rf"({date.pattern})(\s)({time.pattern})")

# Basic regex types
digit = Regex(r"\d")
char = Regex(r"\w")
newline = Regex(r"(\r\n|\r|\n)")  # Matched new lines on Linux, Windows & MacOS
whitespace = Regex(r"\s")

# Document-specific types
sentence = Regex(r"[A-Z].*\s*[.!?]")
paragraph = Regex(rf"{sentence.pattern}(?:\s+{sentence.pattern})*\n+")


# The following regex is FRC 5322 compliant and was found at:
# https://emailregex.com/
email = Regex(
    r"""(?:[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+(?:\.[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+)*|"(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21\x23-\x5b\x5d-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])*")@(?:(?:[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?\.)+[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?|\[(?:(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9]))\.){3}(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9])|[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]:(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21-\x5a\x53-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])+)\])"""
)

# Matches any ISBN number. Note that this is not completely correct as not all
# 10 or 13 digits numbers are valid ISBNs. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISBN
# Taken from O'Reilly's Regular Expression Cookbook:
# https://www.oreilly.com/library/view/regular-expressions-cookbook/9781449327453/ch04s13.html
#
# TODO: The check digit can only be computed by calling a function to compute it dynamically
isbn = Regex(
    r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
)

input.new_string:
isbn = Regex(
    r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
)

# Matches a US Social Security Number in the standard XXX-XX-XXXX format.
# Excludes area numbers 000, 666 and 900-999, and group/serial numbers of
# all zeros, none of which are ever assigned. See:
# https://www.ssa.gov/employer/randomization.html
ssn = Regex(r"(?!000|666|9\d{2})\d{3}-(?!00)\d{2}-(?!0000)\d{4}")

# Excludes area numbers 000, 666 and 900-999, and group/serial numbers of
# all zeros, none of which are ever assigned. See:
# https://www.ssa.gov/employer/randomization.html

tool_use_result.originalFile:
"""Output types for structured generation and regex DSL."""

from dataclasses import dataclass, is_dataclass
from enum import Enum, EnumMeta
from typing import Union

from jsonschema import Draft202012Validator as Validator
from jsonschema.exceptions import SchemaError
from pydantic import BaseModel, TypeAdapter
from typing_extensions import _TypedDictMeta  # type: ignore

from . import airports, countries, locale
from outlines.types.dsl import (
    Regex,
    CFG,
    FSM,
    JsonSchema,
    regex,
    cfg,
    fsm,
    json_schema,
    optional,
    either,
    exactly,
    at_least,
    at_most,
    between,
    zero_or_more,
    one_or_more,
    # deprecated
    repeat,
    times,
)


# Python types
string = Regex(r'"[^"]*"')
integer = Regex(r"[+-]?(0|[1-9][0-9]*)")
boolean = Regex("(True|False)")
number = Regex(rf"{integer.pattern}(\.[0-9]+)?([eE][+-][0-9]+)?")
date = Regex(r"(\d{4})-(0[1-9]|1[0-2])-([0-2][0-9]|3[0-1])")
time = Regex(r"([0-1][0-9]|2[0-3]):([0-5][0-9]):([0-5][0-9])")
datetime = Regex(rf"({date.pattern})(\s)({time.pattern})")

# Basic regex types
digit = Regex(r"\d")
char = Regex(r"\w")
newline = Regex(r"(\r\n|\r|\n)")  # Matched new lines on Linux, Windows & MacOS
whitespace = Regex(r"\s")

# Document-specific types
sentence = Regex(r"[A-Z].*\s*[.!?]")
paragraph = Regex(rf"{sentence.pattern}(?:\s+{sentence.pattern})*\n+")


# The following regex is FRC 5322 compliant and was found at:
# https://emailregex.com/
email = Regex(
    r"""(?:[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+(?:\.[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+)*|"(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21\x23-\x5b\x5d-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])*")@(?:(?:[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?\.)+[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?|\[(?:(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9]))\.){3}(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9])|[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]:(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21-\x5a\x53-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])+)\])"""
)

# Matches any ISBN number. Note that this is not completely correct as not all
# 10 or 13 digits numbers are valid ISBNs. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISBN
# Taken from O'Reilly's Regular Expression Cookbook:
# https://www.oreilly.com/library/view/regular-expressions-cookbook/9781449327453/ch04s13.html
#
# TODO: The check digit can only be computed by calling a function to compute it dynamically
isbn = Regex(
    r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
)

+# Excludes area numbers 000, 666 and 900-999, and group/serial numbers of
+# all zeros, none of which are ever assigned. See:

content.text: Now let's verify it imports and works correctly.

input.command:
python -c "
from outlines.types import ssn
print(ssn.pattern)
import re
p = re.compile(ssn.pattern)
assert p.fullmatch('123-45-6789')
assert not p.fullmatch('000-45-6789')
assert not p.fullmatch('666-45-6789')
assert not p.fullmatch('900-45-6789')
assert not p.fullmatch('123-00-6789')
assert not p.fullmatch('123-45-0000')
print('OK')
"

summary:
python -c "
from outlines.types import ssn
print(ssn.pattern)
import re
p = re.compile(ssn.pattern)
assert p.fullmatch('123-45-6789')
assert not p.fullmatch('000-45-6789')
assert not p.fullmatch('666-45-6789')
assert not p.fullmatch('900-45-6789')
assert not p.fullmatch('123-00-6789')
assert not p.fullmatch('123-45-0000')
print('OK')
"

content.text: Now let's test with a pydantic model as in the usage example, and check other type validation mechanisms.

input.command:
python -c "
from outlines.types import ssn
from outlines.types.dsl import Regex
print(isinstance(ssn, Regex))
print(ssn.to_regex() if hasattr(ssn, 'to_regex') else 'no to_regex method')
"

content.text: Good, it's a proper `Regex` instance consistent with `isbn` and other types. Now generate the patch.

content.content:
diff --git a/outlines/types/__init__.py b/outlines/types/__init__.py
index 85af9adc..2fb7782e 100644
--- a/outlines/types/__init__.py
+++ b/outlines/types/__init__.py
@@ -68,3 +68,9 @@ email = Regex(
 isbn = Regex(
     r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
 )
+
+# Matches a US Social Security Number in the standard XXX-XX-XXXX format.
+# Excludes area numbers 000, 666 and 900-999, and group/serial numbers of
+# all zeros, none of which are ever assigned. See:
+# https://www.ssa.gov/employer/randomization.html
+ssn = Regex(r"(?!000|666|9\d{2})\d{3}-(?!00)\d{2}-(?!0000)\d{4}")

 )
+
+# Matches a US Social Security Number in the standard XXX-XX-XXXX format.
+# Excludes area numbers 000, 666 and 900-999, and group/serial numbers of
+# all zeros, none of which are ever assigned. See:

+ssn = Regex(r"(?!000|666|9\d{2})\d{3}-(?!00)\d{2}-(?!0000)\d{4}")

content.text: Added a new `ssn` type in `outlines/types/__init__.py` right after `isbn`, following the file's existing `Regex(...)` pattern. It validates the standard `XXX-XX-XXXX` SSN format while excluding known-invalid area/group/serial numbers (000, 666, 900-999 area; 00 group; 0000 serial). Verified it imports cleanly and matches/rejects the expected cases. The diff is written to `patch.txt`.

result: Added a new `ssn` type in `outlines/types/__init__.py` right after `isbn`, following the file's existing `Regex(...)` pattern. It validates the standard `XXX-XX-XXXX` SSN format while excluding known-invalid area/group/serial numbers (000, 666, 900-999 area; 00 group; 0000 serial). Verified it imports cleanly and matches/rejects the expected cases. The diff is written to `patch.txt`.
